figures.caption: Fig. 1. Illustration of the overall proposed framework. Input MRI volumes undergo Zscore normalization and feed a Mamba-based encoder–decoder architecture. The predicted output is transformed back to HU via reverse normalization on dataset-level and appropriate clipping. During training, the network is supervised using paired 3D CT volumes with a staged compound loss function

Fig. 2. Visual comparison of U-Net, SwinUNETR, nnU-Net, SegMamba, and UMamba on representative cases for each anatomical site. The target CT images and corresponding MRI images are shown in the last columns
